# UC-15.1.15 · PUE Efficiency Tracking vs Target

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance

*We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.*

---

## Description

Monitors hourly facility kilowatt totals versus IT-only kilowatts normalized under sourcetype "power:aggregate", derives live power usage effectiveness from those readings, and compares the rolling ratio with the annually loaded design scalar pue_design so sustained drift stands out before mechanical teams lose reconciliation with their DCIM baseline.

## Value

Keeping computed PUE inside the contracted design band protects megawatt budgets, avoids surprise cooling headroom shortfalls during consolidation waves, and lets sustainability reporting tie leadership dashboards back to engineered assumptions instead of unexplained utility variance.

## Implementation

Normalize facility_kw, it_load_kw, and pue_design via props.conf on index=power, blend the annually published design target feed described in your DCIM-linked lookup into the aggregate stream, schedule the detection over span=1h buckets, and tune where thresholds seasonally while correlating breaches with recorded facility maintenance windows before paging.

## Evidence

Archive the scheduled PUE Efficiency vs Design saved search CSV plus the linked Dashboard Studio line panel export in a restricted audit_evidence index with thirteen-month retention for facilities governance reviews.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Seed index=power with sourcetype="power:aggregate" rows where facility_kw=8200, it_load_kw=4800, pue_design=1.45, keep FIELDALIAS coverage so total_facility_kw resolves for the catalog SPL, observe rolling eval math near 1.71 for two consecutive hours, and confirm the saved search returns rows surfaced on the PUE Efficiency dashboard panel.

### Negative scenario

Replay the feed but set it_load_kw=25 due to a failed PDU poller while BMS historian trend still shows roughly 4800 kW IT load elsewhere; operators expect silence once a companion data_quality pipeline marks it_meter_stale=Y because denominator collapse should not page without metering_good assurance.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Confirm Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads can reach index=power events carrying sourcetype="power:aggregate" with populated facility_kw, it_load_kw, and pue_design; align aliases so the catalog SPL numerator matches your BMS + IT load meters field naming.
- Where SNMP participates for facility totals or PDU branches, deploy collectors consistent with Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app 6266) or SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app 1537) on a Heavy Forwarder, binding UDP/162 trap or UDP/161 poll stanzas inside inputs.conf and verifying _time skew stays under one poll interval.
- Publish pue_design from the same DCIM-aligned workbook referenced in this UC's implementation summary using a nightly KV Store or CSV lookup refresh orchestrated via savedsearches.conf so mechanical engineers can audit baseline provenance.
- RBAC: provision power_efficiency_analyst with read/search against index=power only and reserve schedule_search for the automation service account executing this detection.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stage props.conf EVAL and FIELDALIAS rules that coerce watts to kilowatts if upstream BMS exports mixed units, stamp site_id or datacenter_code for multi-building estates, and validate Interactive Search over -30d@d shows concurrent facility_kw and it_load_kw samples.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the catalog SPL into Search, wrap it as a savedsearches.conf report with cron_schedule=0 * * * * aligned to dispatch.earliest_time=-65m@m so each hourly bucket completes ingestion, then clone an alert variant that fires only when the post-timechart aggregate shows sustained breach minutes above your paging policy.

#### Understanding this SPL — index=power scopes facility telemetry while sourcetype="power:aggregate" keeps chillers and PDUs in one KPI-friendly stream; eval derives instantaneous PUE; where enforces mechanical tolerance (pue_design * 1.1 plus > 1.6 guardrail); timechart span=1h feeds Dashboard Studio reviewers.

### Step 3 — Validate
Spot-check ratios against BMS trend charts or SNMP PDU consoles for the same interval, reconcile numerator and denominator math whenever DR exercises relocate IT load, and export CSV evidence through Pivot or outputlookup for auditors.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish results on an executive Dashboard Studio view pairing splunk.line live PUE with splunk.singlevalue variance-from-target, route breaches through email or webhook actions documented in docs/runbooks/, and rehearse rollback steps quarterly.

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="power:aggregate"
| eval pue=round(total_facility_kw/it_load_kw,2)
| where pue > pue_design * 1.1 OR pue > 1.6
| timechart span=1h avg(pue) as live_pue
```

## Visualization

Pair a splunk.line hourly series of live_pue with a constant reference band for pue_design and seasonal upper limits, add splunk.singlevalue for trailing thirty-day average delta, use splunk.column for site-level comparisons, and embed splunk.markdown summarizing the baseline revision identifier, lookup freshness timestamp, and mechanical operations owner.

## Known False Positives

Scenario A — Quarterly DR failover swings it_load_kw onto alternate distribution buses while facility_kw still reflects primary chillers; distinguish using maintenance_calendar lookups tagged scenario=drill and suppress paging until workloads normalize. Scenario B — PDU firmware upgrades zero it_load_kw samples briefly even though BMS historian charts show steady racks; distinguish by requiring metering_health=good heartbeats from the IT load meter collector before honoring breaches. Scenario C — Chiller commissioning sequences spike facility_kw without IT movement; correlate chiller_test=Y annotations stored in maintenance_calendar plus manual_override=Y tags and widen thresholds during those windows via seasonal_adjust.csv. Scenario D — Operations publishes stale pue_design after retrofit projects; distinguish false regressions by versioning design_revision and forcing lookups to ignore superseded rows.
